title: Phanera splendens
intro: Phanera splendens, also known as the Indian Mast tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Phanera splendens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phanera splendens is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, with the most common preparations being an infusion made from its leaves and bark, as well as a dried extract used in various supplements.
